metrics.label: CRM completeness
metrics.description: Captures the required company, persona, source, workload, and owner fields that make the record usable.
metrics.suggestion: Fill the buyer role, workload context, and owner before moving the record deeper into the pipeline.

metrics.label: Data hygiene
metrics.description: Keeps the record deduplicated, owned, and tied to a dated next step.
metrics.suggestion: Any active record without an owner or dated next step should be cleaned up immediately.

metrics.label: ICP fit judgment
metrics.description: Shows that the Microsoft workload and business problem actually match the target motion.
metrics.suggestion: Connect the use case explicitly to AVD, Windows 365, Intune, or a multi-workload MSP motion.

metrics.label: Follow-up timeliness
metrics.description: Measures whether the next step is current instead of overdue or forgotten.
metrics.suggestion: Keep active records inside a real cadence and do not let dated next steps age out.

metrics.label: Personalization quality
metrics.description: Rewards outreach that sounds like it understands the account rather than generic desktop automation copy.
metrics.suggestion: Reference the buyer’s actual workload, timing pressure, or operating model in every external touch.

metrics.label: Next-step clarity
metrics.description: Tracks whether the record has a concrete operational next step and owner.
metrics.suggestion: Spell out the next action, who owns it, and when it should happen.

metrics.label: Handoff readiness
metrics.description: Scores whether the SDR captured enough context to pass the opportunity cleanly to the next team.
metrics.suggestion: Do not hand off until the buyer persona, Microsoft fit, activity proof, and next step are all present.

metrics.label: Reporting completeness
metrics.description: Ensures the record is usable in pipeline reviews and weekly summaries.
metrics.suggestion: Keep source, outcome history, and hygiene notes current so the record tells the full story.
